name: Capital Cities
description: World capitals, from the famous to the obscure

questions.q: What is the capital of Australia?

source.notes: Chosen in 1908 as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne. Became capital in 1927.

questions.q: What is the capital of Russia?

source.notes: Home to the Kremlin and Red Square. Population ~12.5 million.

questions.q: Which European city is the capital of Switzerland?

source.notes: Often surprising, as Zurich and Geneva are more famous internationally.

questions.q: What is the capital of South Korea?

source.notes: Hosted 1988 Summer Olympics. Population ~9.8 million city proper.

questions.q: What is the capital of Thailand?

source.notes: Full Thai ceremonial name is the longest city name in the world (~40 words).

questions.q: What is the capital of the Czech Republic?

source.notes: Home to the Prague Castle, the largest ancient castle complex in the world.

questions.q: Which is the highest capital city in the world by elevation?

source.notes: ~3,640 meters (11,942 feet). Bolivia's constitutional capital is Sucre, but La Paz is the seat of government.

questions.q: What was New York City called during its Dutch colonial period?

source.notes: Renamed New York in 1664 after Duke of York.

questions.q: What is the capital of Namibia?

source.notes: Population ~430,000. Former German colonial influence remains.

questions.q: Cardiff is the capital of which country in the United Kingdom?

source.notes: Became the official capital of Wales only in 1955.

questions.q: The modern Turkish city of Istanbul was once known by what name, capital of the Byzantine Empire?

source.notes: Renamed 330 AD after Emperor Constantine. Became Istanbul in 1930.

questions.q: What is the capital of Denmark?

source.notes: The name means 'merchants' harbor.' Home of Tivoli Gardens and the Little Mermaid statue.

questions.q: What is the capital of Portugal?

source.notes: One of the oldest cities in Western Europe, with over 3,000 years of settlement.

questions.q: What is the capital of Norway?

source.notes: Named Christiania from 1624-1925 after King Christian IV.

questions.q: Tehran is the capital of which country?

source.notes: Population ~9 million. Became capital in 1795.

questions.q: What is the capital of Estonia, a digital-savvy Baltic nation?

source.notes: Home of Skype's original development. Medieval Old Town is a UNESCO site.

questions.q: Islamabad is the capital of which country?

source.notes: Planned city. Replaced Karachi as capital in 1967. Name means 'City of Islam.'

questions.q: What is the capital of Belgium, known for waffles and EU headquarters?

source.notes: De facto capital of the EU. Famous for the Grand Place and Manneken Pis.

questions.q: What is the capital of Uruguay?

source.notes: About half of Uruguay's population lives in Montevideo.

questions.q: What is the capital of Mongolia?

source.notes: Also spelled 'Ulan Bator.' One of the coldest capital cities in the world.

questions.q: Taiwan's capital city, known for its 101-floor skyscraper, is what?

source.notes: Taipei 101 was the world's tallest building 2004-2010.
